experiments.description: fix sed regex for safe exceptions — sed -E with separate substitutions and g flag on short flags. REGRESSION: g flag partially matches inside --recursive, causing dangerous rm --recursive commands to be allowed
fix sed substitution order — strip short flags after long flags to prevent partial matching inside --recursive. Same aggregate score but fixes EVAL 3 (0/12 -> 12/12)
strip all --long-flag patterns before short flags in safe-exception sed. Fixes rm -r --force /tmp/important. EVAL 1: 15/15

strip quoted strings (CMD_SAFE) before pattern matching to prevent false positives on echo/grep with destructive keywords. EVAL 5: 6/11 -> 11/11, but EVAL 1 regressed 15/15 -> 14/15 (psql with embedded quotes)
fix JSON extraction truncation — detect trailing backslash in grep output to trigger Python fallback for commands with escaped quotes

SQL client-aware detection — use CMD_SAFE_LOWER for general SQL checks (prevents grep/echo false positives) but also check raw CMD when command starts with a known SQL client (psql, mysql, sqlite3, etc.)

eval_breakdown.name: No false positives on edge cases
